ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the region */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the region in square kilometers */,
  "climate_zone" TEXT /* Climate zone of the region */,
  "governance_type" TEXT /* Type of governance (e.g., city, county, state) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region the dataset belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was collected */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the dataset */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the dataset was last updated */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "housing_data" (
  "housing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each housing data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the housing data belongs to */,
  "median_age" INTEGER /* Median age of the housing */,
  "total_rooms" INTEGER /* Total number of rooms in the housing */,
  "total_bedrooms" INTEGER /* Total number of bedrooms in the housing */,
  "total_bathrooms" INTEGER /* Total number of bathrooms in the housing */,
  "property_type" TEXT /* Type of property (e.g., single-family, apartment) */,
  "year_built" INTEGER /* Year the property was built */,
  "property_value" REAL /* Estimated value of the property */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("housing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_housing_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_data" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the population data belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population */,
  "households" INTEGER /* Total number of households */,
  "average_age" REAL /* Average age of the population */,
  "gender_ratio" REAL /* Ratio of males to females */,
  "ethnicity_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of ethnicities */,
  "employment_rate" REAL /* Employment rate of the population */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "income_data" (
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the income data belongs to */,
  "median_income" REAL /* Median income */,
  "income_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of income */,
  "median_household_income" REAL /* Median household income */,
  "poverty_rate" REAL /* Poverty rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_income_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ocean_proximity" (
  "ocean_proximity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ocean proximity data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the ocean proximity data belongs to */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of ocean proximity (e.g., <1H OCEAN, INLAND, ISLAND, NEAR BAY, NEAR OCEAN) */,
  "distance_to_ocean_km" REAL /* Distance to the nearest ocean in kilometers */,
  "coastal_features" TEXT /* Notable coastal features (e.g., cliffs, beaches) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ocean_proximity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ocean_proximity_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "longitude_data" (
  "longitude_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each longitude data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the longitude data belongs to */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude value */,
  "geographical_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the geographical coordinates */,
  "source_of_coordinates" TEXT /* Source of the coordinates (e.g., GPS, survey) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("longitude_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_longitude_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "latitude_data" (
  "latitude_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each latitude data point */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the latitude data belongs to */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude value */,
  "geographical_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the geographical coordinates */,
  "source_of_coordinates" TEXT /* Source of the coordinates (e.g., GPS, survey) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("latitude_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_latitude_data_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file (e.g., CSV, Excel, JSON) */,
  "file_version" TEXT /* Version of the data file */,
  "file_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the file contents */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to access the dataset (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the version */,
  "version_creator" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_version_creator FOREIGN KEY ("version_creator") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the category belongs to */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_categories_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the tag belongs to */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_tags_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)
